MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER Punitive Damages
Off Calendar May-18-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. Jonathan H. Siegel & Atty. Pawanapreet Dhaliwal of Siegel Lewitter Malkani, Tel. No. 1-510-452-5000 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Elena R. Baca of Paul Hastings, Tel. No. 1-213-683-6306 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - The matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Co.'s motion to bifurcate punitive damages is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The issue can be raised again with the trial judge. Civil Code 3295 does not provide for mandatory bifurcation of all punitive damages issues, only evidence of a defendant's profits or financial condition. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and
to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/ru
